commands.review_summary:
Adds a dispatch-only QA Lab Smoke Profile workflow with manual ref/profile/scenario inputs, while carrying the stacked QA Lab evidence-gallery changes from the related PR.

PR surface: Source +2138, Tests +887, Config +180, Other 0. Total +3205 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a new CI workflow/admin surface. The important missing proof is a real GitHub workflow_dispatch run that exercises the selected QA path and uploads artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This draft needs maintainer choice and runner-dispatch proof, not an automated repair PR.

target.title: ci: add QA Lab smoke profile dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a dispatch-only `QA Lab Smoke Profile` GitHub workflow that runs the existing taxonomy-backed `smoke-ci` QA Lab profile on Ubuntu runners.
- Provides a direct `scenario_id` override for the follow-up UX Matrix scenario so maintainers can dispatch the same workflow against PR 3 once that scenario ID lands.
- Uploads `.artifacts/qa-e2e/` so Evidence Archive can consume `qa-evidence.json`, suite summaries, logs, JSONL, screenshots, and videos from the runner artifact.
- Intentionally avoids editing existing release/smoke workflows, adding mobile simulator/emulator lanes, or changing QA Lab runtime behavior.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #94276
Related #94283

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested as a draft follow-up slice for GitHub Action / smoke-profile wiring after the QA Lab UX Matrix scenario work, with the caveat that the final smoke-ci integration may be renamed or folded into maintainer-owned smoke-ci work.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QA Lab evidence can be dispatched on GitHub runners through the existing `smoke-ci` profile, with an explicit `scenario_id` path reserved for the UX Matrix scenario from PR 3.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on `codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action`, stacked on #94283 head `c0cfa194229c1da502b6da780bb082d33a1d9092`; workflow syntax and QA Lab command surfaces validated locally.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm install`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `PATH="/Users/solvely/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
  - `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml`
  - `OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=0 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa run --help`
  - `OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=0 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite --help`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied local console output from the workflow sanity and QA CLI checks:

```text
$ PATH="/Users/solvely/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" node scripts/check-workflows.mjs
zizmor...................................................................Passed
No direct inputs interpolation found in composite run blocks.

$ go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml
# no output; actionlint exited 0

$ OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=0 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa run --help
Usage: openclaw qa run [options]
Options:
  --category <id>         Limit --qa-profile to a taxonomy category id
  --concurrency <count>   Scenario worker concurrency
  --evidence-mode <mode>  Set profile qa-evidence.json mode: full or slim
  --fast                  Enable provider fast mode where supported (default: false)
  --output-dir <path>     Profile run artifact directory
  --provider-mode <mode>  Provider mode: mock-openai, aimock, live-frontier
  --qa-profile <id>       Run the QA profile from taxonomy.yaml
  --surface <id>          Limit --qa-profile to a taxonomy surface id

$ OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=0 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite --help
Usage: openclaw qa suite [options]
Options:
  --concurrency <count>         Scenario worker concurrency
  --fast                        Enable provider fast mode where supported (default: false)
  --output-dir <path>           Suite artifact directory
  --provider-mode <mode>        Provider mode: mock-openai, aimock, live-frontier
  --scenario <id>               Run only the named QA scenario (repeatable) (default: [])
```

- Observed result after fix: The workflow is syntactically valid and wired only to stable QA Lab CLI surfaces available at the stacked head.
- What was not tested: No GitHub runner dispatch yet, and no UX Matrix scenario run because PR 3 has not provided its final scenario ID/command surface.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This draft is intentionally a wiring slice; default execution is headless/mock-provider only and avoids live services, Android emulators, and iOS simulators.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing release QA workflows can run QA Lab lanes, but there was no small dispatch-only runner entry point for smoke profile evidence or the upcoming UX Matrix scenario override.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm install`
- `git diff --check`
- `PATH="/Users/solvely/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
- `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml`
- `OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=0 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa run --help`
- `OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=0 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite --help`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

No runtime tests were added; this is workflow/config wiring over existing QA Lab CLI contracts.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The missing piece was not a failing code path; it was the absence of a dispatchable runner path for smoke-profile/UX Matrix QA evidence artifacts.

If no test was added, why not?

The workflow validates through the repo workflow sanity script, actionlint, and the existing QA Lab command surfaces. A real runner dispatch should be the next proof step once maintainers choose whether to keep this dedicated draft workflow or fold it into the planned smoke-ci action.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No runtime config or migration behavior changed. This adds a manually dispatched GitHub workflow.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly: a new manually dispatched workflow can run QA Lab commands on GitHub-hosted runners with read-only contents permissions and live provider keys scrubbed.

What is the highest-risk area?

Workflow ownership/shape may overlap with maintainer-owned smoke-ci work.

How is that risk mitigated?

The workflow is draft, dispatch-only, does not rename or replace existing workflows, uses the existing `smoke-ci` QA profile by default, and exposes `scenario_id` as the minimal PR 3 integration target.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review as a draft wiring slice and decide whether to keep this as an initial UX Matrix action, rename it, or fold the command shape into the planned smoke-ci workflow/profile.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on PR 3 to provide the final UX Matrix scenario ID. The expected command surface is `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario <ux-matrix-scenario-id> --concurrency 1 --fast --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/<ux-matrix-scenario-id>`, producing `qa-evidence.json` and any screenshots/videos/logs under that output directory.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

The PR body now includes copied console output for the Real behavior proof gate.
